text: Literally, ‘on behalf of the kingdom, of the sanctuary, and of Juda’.

Ezechias talks of this temple cleansing as if it had been, in effect, a reconsecration of the priesthood. In this and the following verses it is necessary to distinguish between the burnt-sacrifice, which was devoted entirely to the Lord (Lev. 1), and the welcome-offering, which was shared between the Lord and his worshipper (Lev. 3).
The sense of the Hebrew text is rather that the Levites had been more energetic than the priests in going through the ceremony of personal purification.
